title: taraxacum andiniforme
intro: Taraxacum andiniforme, also known as Andean dandelion, is a perennial plant species native to South America, characterized by its yellow flowers and jagged leaves. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Taraxacum andiniforme include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support liver function. Herbal preparations of Taraxacum andiniforme can be consumed as tea, capsules, or extracts, and are often used to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and promote overall well-being.
